title: centaurea uniflora subsp. ferdinandi
intro: Centaurea uniflora subsp. ferdinandi, also known as the Ferdinand's Knapweed, is a plant species within the genus Centaurea that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Centaurea uniflora subsp. ferdinandi can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
